metadata.name: Human / Consumer Comparator Tree
metadata.purpose: External TreeView data source for consumer value comparison, offers/listings, bids, records, and transaction learning.
source_notes: COMPARATOR-TREE-spaced.TXT used as the primary legacy source because it is the cleanest indentation source.
COMPARATOR-TREE-1.TXT used as a backup/cross-check; missing branches such as Road Vehicles > Motorcycles and Offroad Vehicles were merged.
Brand files are stored under brand_lookups only and are not expanded as visible TreeView nodes.
Type files were used to enrich lower-level consumer product/service classes where logical.
Object folders for Offers / Listings, Bids, and Records are provided through reusable template OFT_STANDARD_COMPARATOR_CLASS rather than duplicated under every comparable class.
Models, Evidence, and VRQs are deliberately not visible standard TreeView children; they belong to activity tools, record attachments/evidence, and message/event streams.

value_formula_context.notes: Value formula source files were used as context for common comparison fields and risk/evidence/grouping support, not as visible tree nodes.

nodes.label: Human / Consumer
nodes.notes: Root sector for the Human / Consumer Comparator TreeView.

warnings: Breathable Air is synthetic/user-requested and needs future refinement for air rights, public air quality, home filtration, and medical oxygen boundaries.
Health (Non Nutrutional) was normalized to Medical; some spiritual/emotional/nutritional items remain in the legacy branch and may need health-domain cleanup.
Wealth was normalized to Financial; legacy products such as Money Collections, Businesses, and Income may overlap with investment/business views.
Government was normalized to Public / Government; rights, representation, and services were preserved but some are reference objects rather than market offers.
Communication was normalized from Communications; phone/device type enrichment was added as consumer purchasable classes.

Business & Industry is retained from the consumer viewpoint as a person/household accessing industrial/business goods, services, franchises, or businesses for sale; not a full seller-side business taxonomy.

Legacy regulated/sensitive items such as Alcohol, Weapons, Strippers, insurance, banking, medical devices, and legal services remain as taxonomy classes and need permission/regulatory overlays before operational use.
Legacy speculative items such as UFO, Flying Saucer, Zero Point, Plasmic, and GeoHarmonic were preserved for continuity but should be reviewed before public release.
Some source type files contain cross-category products or broad retail catalog noise; logical placements were chosen but may need human pruning.
